description: Install Adapty packages, remove RevenueCat packages, and update app.json with Adapty Expo plugins.
steps: Run: cd /projects/sandbox/Vocolens-app && npx expo install react-native-adapty adaptyui-react-native --fix
Remove RevenueCat packages: npm uninstall react-native-purchases react-native-purchases-ui --legacy-peer-deps
In app.json, add 'react-native-adapty' and 'adaptyui-react-native' to the plugins array (as simple string entries after 'expo-updates')
In package.json, verify that react-native-purchases and react-native-purchases-ui are gone from dependencies
Create a .env.example file at project root with content: EXPO_PUBLIC_ADAPTY_KEY=your_public_sdk_key
acceptance_criteria: package.json contains react-native-adapty and adaptyui-react-native in dependencies
package.json does NOT contain react-native-purchases or react-native-purchases-ui
verification: Run: cd /projects/sandbox/Vocolens-app && cat package.json | grep -E 'adapty|react-native-purchases' to confirm correct packages
Run: cd /projects/sandbox/Vocolens-app && cat app.json | grep adapty to confirm plugins
findings: The package 'adaptyui-react-native' does not exist on npm. The Adapty UI module was deprecated and merged into react-native-adapty since v3.3.1+. Only react-native-adapty (v3.15.6) is needed - it includes both SDK and UI functionality. Only one plugin entry ('react-native-adapty') is needed in app.json since it bundles both. Pre-existing TypeScript errors exist in the project (23 errors) unrelated to this change. The .gitignore has multiple .env.* patterns that block .env.example; used git add -f to force-add it.
